landing.hero_desc: Welcome to the official cooperative tracking portal. We facilitate collaborative financial pools, low-interest credit unions, and secure auditing utilizing a dual-control workflow structure.
landing.about_desc: Designed to optimize governance, accelerate development, and uphold the highest tier of financial compliance.
landing.workflow_desc: Complete division of powers between Secretaries who initiate transactions, and Treasurers who authorize pool verification and confirm payouts.
landing.interest_desc: Our innovative model calculates loan interest purely on the borrower's contributed shares instead of outstanding debt.
landing.gateways_desc: Four distinct user levels (Admin, Treasurer, Secretary, Member) ensuring customized interfaces, secure route protection, and full data compartmentalization.
landing.security_desc: TEBAMS employs state-of-the-art security protocols. All user passwords are encrypted using industry-standard bcrypt hashing, routes are protected by role-based guardrails, and every database modification undergoes mandatory double-entry verification to eliminate fraud.
landing.storage_desc: Your cooperative records are hosted in a secure central PostgreSQL database with encrypted TLS connection pipelines. Additionally, local transactions are safely queued inside secure device-level IndexedDB sandboxes for offline capability.
landing.privacy_desc: We maintain strict privacy controls. Financial records are strictly compartmentalized, making them accessible only to the owner, authorized Secretaries, and Treasurers. Every single data access is cataloged in our immutable Audit Trail.
landing.faq_q1: What is Teachers Bank Management System (TEBAMS)?
landing.faq_a1: TEBAMS is a secure cooperative financial tracking platform designed specifically for managing educators' share contributions, emergency funds, and organizational loans. It ensures complete transparency and accountability.
landing.faq_q2: How does the dual-control financial workflow work?
landing.faq_a2: To maintain financial integrity, TEBAMS uses a strict two-person control system. The Secretary registers members and records all transactions (contributions, repayments, loan requests) as PENDING. The Treasurer then reviews, verifies, and officially confirms or rejects them.
landing.faq_q3: How is loan interest calculated in the system?
landing.faq_a3: Unlike traditional commercial lending where interest is based on the loan principal, TEBAMS calculates loan interest based on the member's individual contributed shares. This model incentivizes cooperative savings over debt.
landing.faq_q4: What access level do cooperative members have?
landing.faq_a4: Cooperative members are granted personal access keys allowing them to log in and securely audit their own history, including confirmed share pools, outstanding loans, repayments, and provisional receipts.
landing.faq_q5: Can the system work without an active internet connection?
landing.faq_a5: Yes! TEBAMS is built as a Progressive Web App (PWA) with built-in offline capability. You can register members, log contributions, and view records completely offline. All actions are securely queued in local IndexedDB and automatically synchronized with the main database as soon as connection is restored.
landing.faq_q6: How secure is my financial data in this system?
landing.faq_a6: TEBAMS employs multiple layers of security, including role-based routing barriers, cryptographic hashing of passwords, and secure session management. Crucially, the dual-control model ensures no single individual can record and approve transactions alone, blocking internal fraud.
landing.faq_q7: Who is eligible to use the system?
landing.faq_a7: TEBAMS is specifically designed for teachers' savings cooperatives, credit unions, and educator-led financial pools. Registered educators, school coordinators, secretaries, treasurers, and cooperative administrators are fully eligible to access their respective secure portals.
landing.faq_q8: What is the step-by-step workflow per system role?
landing.faq_a8: The workflow is role-based: 1) Admin configures cooperative terms, dynamic currencies, and manages system users. 2) Secretary registers members, logs contributions, records loan requests, and repayments (marked as pending). 3) Treasurer verifies pending transactions, approves loans, and confirms cash payouts. 4) Member audits personal share logs, active loans, and downloads real-time PDF receipts.

privacy.subtitle: How we protect your data, ensure compliance, and maintain absolute confidentiality.
privacy.legal_p1: We are committed to ensuring that your information is secure. In order to prevent unauthorised access or disclosure, we have put in place suitable physical, electronic and managerial procedures to safeguard and secure the information we collect.
privacy.legal_p2: All personal data is processed in accordance with applicable data protection regulations. Data is never shared with third parties without explicit consent, except where required by law.
privacy.legal_p3: You have the right to request access to the personal data we hold about you, request correction of inaccurate data, and request deletion of your data subject to legal obligations.
privacy.legal_p4: For any privacy-related concerns, please contact us at

faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about how the system works.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.subtitle: Please read these terms carefully before using the platform.
terms.sec1_body: By accessing or using this system, you confirm that you have read, understood, and agree to be bound by these Terms of Service. If you do not agree to these terms, you must not use this platform.
terms.sec2_body: Access to this system is restricted to authorized personnel of the cooperative organization. User accounts are assigned by the system administrator. Sharing account credentials is strictly prohibited.
terms.sec3_body: Each user role (Admin, Treasurer, Secretary, Member) carries defined permissions and responsibilities. Users are expected to perform only the actions permitted by their assigned role. Unauthorized attempts to access restricted modules will be logged and reviewed.
terms.sec4_body: All financial transactions recorded in the system operate under a dual-control workflow. Transactions initiated by a Secretary must be independently verified and confirmed by a Treasurer. Neither party may approve their own entries.
terms.sec5_body: All users are responsible for ensuring the accuracy of the data they input. Deliberate entry of false financial records is a serious breach of these Terms and may result in immediate account suspension and legal action.
terms.sec6_body: All system actions are recorded in an immutable Audit Trail. This log includes the user identity, timestamp, action type, and outcome. This trail is accessible exclusively to the system administrator.
terms.sec7_body: All financial data accessed through this system is strictly confidential. Users must not disclose, share, or reproduce any organizational or member financial information outside of authorized official channels.
terms.sec8_body: The system administrator reserves the right to suspend or terminate any user account found to be in violation of these Terms of Service, without prior notice.
terms.sec9_body: These Terms of Service may be updated from time to time. Continued use of the system following any such changes constitutes your acceptance of the new terms.
terms.sec10_body_1: For any questions regarding these Terms, contact the system support team at
